Avtomat Kalashnikova aka AK-47

# Avtomat Kalashnikova aka AK-47

### The most popular assault rifle

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Nov 13, 2020

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

AK-47 one of the world’s most popular assault rifles, that has become a part of common discourse, used in umpteen conflicts across the world. The inspiration for this rifle was the German StG 44 used in World War II, that they used with devastating effect. The Red Army at that time had to make do with the rather outdated, PPSh-41 SMGs and Mosin–Nagant bolt-action rifles.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!aNwF!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8d43cbe9-6166-407c-b0e8-1351b93c4c77_300x88.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!aNwF!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8d43cbe9-6166-407c-b0e8-1351b93c4c77_300x88.jpeg)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ABkG!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F79f9e8d1-ff32-4bd2-8083-d4f0db7b9636_300x222.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ABkG!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F79f9e8d1-ff32-4bd2-8083-d4f0db7b9636_300x222.jpeg)

Around the same time, a Russian general was recuperating from the wounds he had received at the Battle of Bryansk, when another soldier asked him “Why do we not have automatics like the Germans do, why should 4-5 soldiers have the same weapon?”. And that set the General, who was a self taught weaponry designer to come up with their own version. That man was none other than Mikhail Kalashnikov, after whom the rifle would be named.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!CMfY!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8e94bf40-40b6-495c-89b5-6339f7e33c74_360x322.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!CMfY!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8e94bf40-40b6-495c-89b5-6339f7e33c74_360x322.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!K-Aj!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fc21874f5-a578-4655-963c-5f784505404c_680x383.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!K-Aj!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fc21874f5-a578-4655-963c-5f784505404c_680x383.jpeg)

The name Avtomat Kalashnikova literally translates to Kalashnikov's automated assault rifle and with it's completion in 1947, it got it's more popular name.

**So what made this assault rifle so popular?**

It was due to the fact that it was designed to be a simple, easy to manufacture rifle, using mass production methods, and could operate under any condition.

AK-47 uses a long stroke gas system. Basically what happens in any assault rifle is that the operator, puts in a loaded magazine,releases the charging handle and then pulls the trigger. In a full automatic rifle, fresh rounds are fired continously into the chamber, until the magazine is totally exhausted. . What happens in an AK-47 is that the gas expanding behind the traveling bullet is diverted into a gas chamber, where it in turn acts on a gas piston.

The piston pushes the bolt backwards, ejecting the spent rounds. The advantage here is that the long stroke gas system can operate in any sort of extreme weather conditions be it snow, heavy rain or desert. Also it is more economic, and pretty simple to make, does not involve large costs of production, all you need are some machines to make the receiver which are quite simple.

So even if you really have a pretty primitive or half decent industrial infrastructure, the AK-47 is easy to manufacture, without incurring too many costs. This is the reason why it was the preferred choice for insurgents and rebels, in most 3rd world nations. One more factor is that with no patent rights, any one can tweak the basic design of AK-47 a bit, and customize it to suit the local conditions. And this made it available all over the globe. Another factor was the ease of use, unlike other assault rifles, that needed trained military personnel, AK-47 could be operated by just about any one.

So if you were some dirt poor peasant, in Africa or Latin America, drawn into one of those numerous Cold War era insurgencies, the AK-47 was the best option for you. And yeah it was goddamn sturdy. The Soviet Union played no small role in popularizing the AK-47, their most popular export after vodka. In which ever nation the Soviet Union wanted to spread revolution against "imperialist, bourgeois, capitalist" forces, they just flooded it with AK-47's.
